qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in ESM module loading allows arbitrary JavaScript execution outside configured directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns ESM module path traversal in importModuleRespectingNodePath via path.join and import(); the advisory concerns a worker protocol race from missing request-response correlation that can return stale data to another caller.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described worker protocol race or missing request-response correlation. The only final qualifying finding is an unrelated ESM module path traversal in the same package.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-rwcr-rpcc-3g9m describes cross-user data leakage caused by stale responses in the elixir-nodejs worker protocol due to lack of request-response correlation.
The final workflow manifest lists only path_traversal_esm_module_loading as a qualifying finding.

path_traversal_esm_module_loading/vulnerability_report.json identifies importModuleRespectingNodePath in priv/server.js and path.join(nodePath, modulePath) traversal into import(), not worker response correlation or stale buffered responses.
